normalized_definitions.experimental_test_summary: A high-level summary of the overall experimental procedure, including test type, solution chemistry, operating parameters, and environmental conditions.
normalized_definitions.pitting_potential: The specific extracted critical potential value, including units of measure, at which stable localized pitting initiation occurs.
normalized_definitions.reference_electrode: The standard reference electrode (e.g., SCE, Ag/AgCl) used during electrochemical testing to measure and standardize potentials.
normalized_definitions.polarization_scan_rate: The continuous sweep rate or discrete step-size and holding duration applied to change the electrical potential during polarization testing.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_temperature: The explicitly controlled temperature of the test solution or electrolyte during the measurement, representing a critical thermodynamic variable that influences pitting.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_composition: The precise chemical formulation, specific solvent, molar concentrations, and added inhibitors of the testing solution or electrolyte representing the aggressive environment driving corrosion.
normalized_definitions.aeration_status: The specific condition of dissolved reactive gases in the electrolyte, including whether the solution was aerated, naturally exposed to air, or deaerated/purged with an inert gas like nitrogen or argon.
normalized_definitions.electrochemical_test_method: The specific suite of electrochemical testing methodologies applied (e.g., potentiodynamic sweeps, potentiostatic holds) that dictate the test severity and resulting polarization parameters.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_pH: The measured acidity or alkalinity of the test solution prior to or during the experiment, acting as a critical thermodynamic parameter influencing passive film stability.
normalized_definitions.exposed_testing_area: The precise surface area of the working electrode exposed to the electrolyte, which impacts the statistical probability of encountering pit-initiating structural defects.
normalized_definitions.pitting_initiation_criterion: The quantitative experimental threshold (e.g., a specific current density spike) or qualitative methodology (e.g., visual or microscopic confirmation) used to definitively record the onset of stable pitting.
normalized_definitions.critical_pitting_temperature: The explicitly measured critical temperature threshold (CPT) below which pit initiation does not occur, or above which stable localized pitting initiates in a standardized testing environment.
normalized_definitions.crevice_corrosion_temperature: The minimum critical temperature (CCT) at which crevice corrosion initiates under a specific artificial crevice configuration in a standardized testing solution.
normalized_definitions.crevice_former_type: The specific materials and mechanical setups used to simulate occluded cell geometries and built-in crevices for crevice corrosion testing.
normalized_definitions.exposure_duration: The precise length of continuous immersion time or incubation period the specimen is exposed to the testing environment at a given parameter or isothermal step.
normalized_definitions.corrosion_rate: The quantitative rate of active corrosion determined during the test, typically calculated via measured weight loss or electrochemistry and expressed in units such as inches per month or mm/year.
normalized_definitions.volume_to_surface_area_ratio: The controlled minimum volume of test electrolyte per unit of exposed specimen surface area required to maintain bulk solution chemistry stability during corrosion testing.
normalized_definitions.crevice_corrosion_potential: The specific electrochemical potential at which stable crevice corrosion initiates, distinct from open-surface pitting potential, measured using specimens with engineered crevice formers.
normalized_definitions.ocp_stabilization_time: The required duration for which the specimen is immersed in the electrolyte to establish a steady-state Open Circuit Potential (OCP) prior to active polarization.
normalized_definitions.corrosion_potential: The steady-state open-circuit or free corrosion potential (Ecorr) where the rate of anodic dissolution equals the rate of cathodic reduction, serving as the thermodynamic baseline prior to breakdown.
normalized_definitions.corrosion_current_density: The calculated kinetic rate of corrosion at the corrosion potential, acting as a fundamental metric for general corrosion resistance prior to localized breakdown.
normalized_definitions.passive_window_range: The specific voltage range defined as the difference between the pitting potential and the corrosion potential (Epit - Ecorr), characterizing the span over which the material maintains a stable passive film.
normalized_definitions.counter_electrode: The material used for the auxiliary or counter electrode (e.g., platinum) in the electrochemical cell setup, necessary to complete the circuit and validate the methodology.
normalized_definitions.polarization_scan_range: The specific electrical potential boundaries of the potentiodynamic polarization test, detailing the initial starting voltage and the final anodic potential limit.
normalized_definitions.agitation_conditions: The hydrodynamic state of the electrolyte during testing (e.g., stagnant or stirred), which significantly impacts mass transport dynamics and concentration polarization near the sample surface.
normalized_definitions.electrochemical_pre_conditioning: Details of any in-situ potentiostatic or galvanostatic treatments applied immediately prior to the main scan, such as a cathodic hold to reduce native surface oxides.
